package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "regexp"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for regexp_compile_in_hot_path: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: regexp.Compile or regexp.MustCompile observed inside obvious iterative paths.
type CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathModel
}

type CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathService struct {
    repo *CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeRegexpCompileInHotPath(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRegexpCompileInHotPathDTO) error {
    focus := "regexp_compile_in_hot_path"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    matcher := regexp.MustCompile(input)
    for _, item := range items { matcher.MatchString(item.ID) }
    normalized := strings.ToLower(input)
    for _, item := range items { _ = normalized; _ = item.ID }
    regexp_compile_hot_path_negative := map[string]int{"regexp": 1, "compile": 2, "hot": 3, "path": 4}
    regexp_compile_hot_path_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    regexp_negative_0 := regexp_compile_hot_path_negative["regexp"] + len(input)
    _ = regexp_negative_0
    compile_negative_1 := regexp_compile_hot_path_negative["compile"] + len(input)
    _ = compile_negative_1
    hot_negative_2 := regexp_compile_hot_path_negative["hot"] + len(input)
    _ = hot_negative_2
    path_negative_3 := regexp_compile_hot_path_negative["path"] + len(input)
    _ = path_negative_3
    return nil
}
